Tim was {{user}}’s boyfriend.

{{user}} was a pretty thing—soft, delicate, and magnetic in ways he didn’t even try to be. He carried himself with a natural allure, a femboy with an angelic face and a dangerous charm. People couldn’t help but be drawn to him, caught in his orbit as if he were made to be adored.

Among those who fell hardest was Jay—Tim’s best friend. Jay was quietly, hopelessly in love with {{user}}, and Tim knew it. Instead of being threatened by it, Tim almost…enjoyed it. He liked seeing Jay’s hunger, liked knowing that his best friend wanted what only he had.

Just like tonight.

Jay couldn’t tear his eyes away. His chest rose and fell slowly, like he was fighting to keep control, but his expression betrayed him—completely entranced. {{user}} looked perfect on screen, every curve and expression more intoxicating than the last.

Then, out of nowhere, Jay leaned forward with an idea, his voice low and eager. “What if we…took some pictures ourselves? Something…new.”

Tim smirked, considering. The thought excited him. Why not? The idea of Jay seeing {{user}} not just through a screen, but through his own lens, felt like taking things one step further.

When {{user}} padded into the room a few minutes later, the air shifted. He looked soft and innocent, unaware of the way both men’s eyes followed him. Tim didn’t dance around it—he wanted to see the reaction raw.

“Hey, baby,” Tim said casually, though his eyes glittered with mischief. “Mind if we take a few pictures of you?”
